samples.xml_description:
Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers. Clock signal. Reset signal. Signed or unsigned operation. Input operand DIVIDEND. Input operand DIVISOR. Valid operation request. The output has been acknowledged. The result is valid and ready. Output result. 0 1 2 3 4 5 Count cycles. Start count. Remainder. Quotient. Negated divisor. Multiplexer selector. Substract result. Shift register. Result valid.

Start the operation when opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, save the input operands.

Initialize the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.

Set NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor.

Set the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.

When start_cnt is high, perform the following steps:

If the counter cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete.

  1. Reset start_cnt to 0.
  2. Set the rem to the SR[16:1] and q to the SR[0], which means the rem and q.
  3. Clear the SR.
  4. If cnt is not 8, increment the cnt.

Update the shift register SR with the subtraction result:

  1. Calculate the subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR.
  2. Use a multiplexer to select the result based on the carry-out.
  3. Shift the SR to the left and insert the carry-out.

Manage the res_valid signal based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed.

  • Set res_valid to 0 when rst is high.
  • Set res_valid to 1 when cnt reaches 8.
  • Set res_valid to 0 when res_valid and res_ready is all high.
